questions.question: In this training, a complaint is best understood as which of the following?
options: A nuisance to be managed with as little fuss as possible
A legal claim that must immediately be passed to a solicitor
A personal attack on the worker who receives it
A gift — honest feedback and a chance to put things right

questions.question: Which regulations set the main rules for a lawful complaints procedure in adult social care in England?
options: The Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
The Local Authority Social Services and NHS Complaints (England) Regulations 2009

questions.question: What is the title of CQC Regulation 16?
Under the 2009 Regulations, within what time must a complaint normally be acknowledged?
options: Three working days of receiving it
The same working day, without exception

questions.question: Generally, how long does a person have to make a complaint after the event, or after becoming aware of it?
options: There is no time limit at all

questions.question: The statutory duty of candour is set out in which CQC regulation?
According to the training, what is the reason many complaints escalate?
options: Because the final response was sent in writing
Because the complaint was recorded too quickly

questions.question: How is NHS Complaints Advocacy described in the material?
options: A branch of the CQC that inspects providers
A paid legal service for pursuing court claims
A free service available to help people make a complaint about health or social care
A service that is only available to staff who blow the whistle

questions.question: Which records may a CQC inspector ask to see as evidence of a safe, well-led service?
Of the four routes that are easy to confuse, which must always take priority and be reported straight away, never treated as 'just' a complaint?
Before taking a complaint to the Local Government and Social Care Ombudsman, what should a person normally do first?
options: Post about it on social media
Pay a fee to the Ombudsman
Complete the service's own complaints process

questions.question: Which of these is a remedy the Local Government and Social Care Ombudsman can recommend?
options: Removal of the provider's CQC registration
Criminal prosecution of a care worker

questions.question: If a service has not responded within a reasonable time, a person can generally approach the Ombudsman after around how many weeks?
Which of the following best describes whistleblowing?
options: An informal worry that can be sorted out there and then
A worker raising a concern in the public interest about wrongdoing or serious risk at work
A person expressing dissatisfaction about the care they have received
A family member asking about visiting times

questions.question: The CQC can ask to see a service's complaints records as evidence of a safe, well-led service.
The 2009 Regulations set a single fixed deadline that every final complaint response must meet.
Anyone can ask for an independent advocate to help them make a complaint.
If a person cannot easily tell you about a complaint, it is safe to assume they do not have one.
Under the 2009 Regulations, what must a lawful complaints procedure do? (Select all that apply)
options: Make it easy for people to complain
Name a responsible person who oversees the process
Filter out complaints that seem trivial
Guarantee the complainant a specific outcome in advance

questions.question: Which of the following help make complaining genuinely accessible? (Select all that apply)
options: Only accepting complaints that are put in writing
Requiring every complaint to be made within three days
Providing information in easy-read, large print or translated formats
Reassuring people that complaining will not affect their care

questions.question: In the spirit of the duty of candour, what should a good response do when something has gone wrong? (Select all that apply)
options: Deny any error to avoid admitting liability
Say what will change to prevent it happening again

questions.question: Which steps should every complaint follow from receiving to responding? (Select all that apply)
A widely used approach to receiving a complaint can be remembered by the acronym ___: Listen, Empathise, Apologise, React, Notify.
Some people will need help from an independent ___ to make sure their complaint is heard.
Telling people what changed as a result of their feedback is often summed up as 'you said, we ___'.
